alternatives_rejected: Rate the legacy in-process throttle delay (P2-watch) as a P2 -- rejected: doc 10 Slack Dedupe And Suppression forbids SUPPRESSING (dropping/coalescing-away) a first stop/escalate; the legacy _digest_packet_to_send only DELAYS such a digest by at most one min_digest_interval_seconds poll and re-emits it via pending_due, after which decide()'s never-suppress arm fires -- the digest still SENDS, so it is not a suppression; recorded as the non-blocking P3-10d-2-1 instead.

Rate the _packet_recommended_route heuristic (P2-route) as a P2 -- rejected: the derived route feeds only the dedupe KEY; a wrong derivation can only make the key MORE distinct (a spurious new first-seen key -> a spurious SEND), it cannot collapse two genuinely-different states onto one SENT key in a way that suppresses a real escalation, and the never-suppress arms key off whether THIS key is first-seen -- so a wrong route causes only a spurious send, never a missed escalation (the conservative direction); the Slice-10e step-4 evidence_mode wiring will give the packet a cleaner typed control_plane fact.

Treat the updated test test_supervisor_digest_compose_failure_marks_pending_claim_failed as a weakening -- rejected: every original assertion is kept and one is added; the pre-10d-2 test ran with NO dedupe store so a non-escalation background digest reached the compose path directly, whereas post-10d-2 the doc-10-correct behavior is fail-quiet, so the test was updated to wire a working dedupe store (the realistic production config) where a first-seen digest decides should_send=True and the compose path is still reached -- the genuine behavior change is correctly tested, no safety/boundary/reason-code coverage dropped.

decision: Slice 11 begins with sub-slice 11a: the lowest-risk extraction (pure data types) that proves the import-shim pattern without any behavior risk. The pattern established by 11a is the template for every later 11.x extraction: each PR is refactor-only unless explicitly the final atomic-landing step (11.13 in doc 11; absent here per the construction-vs-rollout distinction), each PR carries a four-question answer + a shim audit + a minimum proof bundle, and each PR's extracted module never imports back into implementation.py.
alternatives_rejected: Start Slice 11 with git_service.py instead of types.py -- REJECTED because doc 11 Refactoring Steps explicitly order types first so later extractions can preserve monkeypatch + import compatibility; types are the dependency leaves that everything else imports.

Move DagArtifactClosureScan and _dedupe_preserving_order together in 11a -- REJECTED because _dedupe_preserving_order has many non-type call sites in implementation.py and moving it broadens the sub-slice scope into non-type code; the prompt's hard rule 'do not move classes that have method bodies depending on outside helpers' applies; defer DagArtifactClosureScan to a later sub-slice that owns the helper.

Move all the private routing constants (_COMMIT_HYGIENE_ROUTE etc.) to types.py too -- REJECTED because they are not part of a TYPE contract; they are routing-helper constants. Only the one default-value constant for DagAuthorityGateOutcome.route would qualify, and we inline that as a literal to avoid duplicating a constant whose primary uses are in routing helpers we are not moving.

Land Slice 11 as one big atomic refactor PR -- REJECTED because doc 11 explicitly orders the extractions and requires per-PR proof bundles + targeted-test gates; staging via small extraction PRs keeps each PR independently reversible and bounded-risk.

The 'unused toggles' clause targets construction-only flags accumulated during refactor; no such flags exist in the Slice 11/12 surface area.

Catch ControlPlaneAdoptionError specifically in Slack _resume_workflow and add new error-handling logic -- rejected: the existing broad except Exception at orchestrator.py:1037 ALREADY handles the error cleanly (posts the 'Resume failed' message; keeps feature in _recoverable_features for retry; NO silent fallback).
